profile.name: Param Pabari
profile.description: Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay with a minor in AI & Data Science. Direct experience across venture investing, business strategy, and startup operations.
profile.bio: I'm Param Pabari â a second-year undergraduate at IIT Bombay studying Mechanical Engineering with a minor in AI & Data Science. What drives me is the intersection of capital, strategy, and execution. I spend most of my time evaluating startups, building investment theses, and working on live deal pipelines. Outside of finance, I've led large-scale technical programming on campus, mentored juniors, and competed in hockey at the institute level. I believe the best way to learn is to operate â with real stakes, real deadlines, and real accountability.

bullets: Working on business strategy priorities for a D2C health brand with INR 237 Cr FY24 revenue.
Parallel execution with live investment analyst responsibilities.
Supporting live deal evaluation and sourcing at a VC fund with INR 250 Cr AUM.

Received Letter of Recommendation from the AGM.

Created first external fund partnerships with YPoint Capital and RSPN Ventures.
Managing INR 15L+ live student investment fund targeting 15% CAGR.
Led IIT Bombay's first DSA bootcamp with Coding Ninjas â 800+ students in 6 weeks.
Mentoring 6 sophomores on academics, prioritization, and co-curricular strategy.

posts.title: A Practical Memo On Diagnostics Roll-Up Economics
posts.summary: What changes when diagnostics is modeled as a service-node roll-up rather than a loose asset basket.
posts.content:
A diagnostics thesis only works when service density, referral pathways, and test mix are modeled together. In fragmented markets, simple top-line multiplication often hides execution drag.

What moved the analysis

The highest signal came from unit economics at node level and not from aggregate market-size claims. Modeling acquisition at lower multiples and exits at strategic-scale multiples created a tighter view of risk-adjusted return.

For investors, the practical question is not market size alone. It is whether integration velocity and quality consistency can compound before capital intensity erodes optionality.

posts.title: How RBI Rate Cycles Shape Equity Rotation
posts.summary: Twelve cycles suggest policy effects show up with a lag, but markets reprice expectations earlier.
posts.content:
Across major tightening and easing windows since 2013, policy transmission to real demand has generally shown a multi-quarter lag. That lag creates a timing gap between narrative and fundamentals.

Where this is useful

For portfolio construction, the key is sequencing. Bond repricing tends to move first, while equity leadership rotates only after confidence on earnings durability improves.

The implication is simple: avoid one-step conclusions from policy announcements, and treat each sector by balance-sheet resilience and pricing power.

posts.title: Building A Student Investment Fund Risk Framework
posts.summary: How to keep conviction high while preserving downside control in an educational portfolio setting.
posts.content:
Student funds fail when process is replaced by opinion. A robust framework starts with allocation guardrails, review cadence, and explicit kill criteria.

Core rules

  • Position sizing should reflect uncertainty and not excitement.
  • Each thesis must have a disconfirming signal defined in advance.
  • Post-mortems are mandatory for both winners and losers.

The objective is to train judgement under pressure, not to chase isolated outcomes.
